description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is made of dark metal, featuring ornate engravings and a flared muzzle, and is securely fastened to the carriage with metal brackets. The carriage is constructed from heavy wooden planks, reinforced with metal bands and bolts, and sits atop four solid wooden wheels with metal rims. The overall design is robust, with the cannon barrel extending well beyond the front of the carriage, and a small handle or knob at the rear for aiming adjustments. The wheels are thick and sturdy, suitable for rolling the cannon short distances on relatively flat surfaces. The object exudes a sense of weight and durability, typical of artillery pieces used in historical urban fortifications or military displays.
description: A heavy, ornate metal cannon mounted on a reinforced wooden wheeled carriage, featuring detailed engravings and robust construction. The design is reminiscent of historical artillery used in urban defense.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cannon's muzzle, metal barrel, and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the cannon barrel, the detailed engravings, and the full profile of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage, the rear end of the cannon barrel, and the handle or knob for aiming.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the other side of the cannon and carriage details.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed large-caliber cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid black and brown.
